attack_title: Potential consensus bug in ALTBN128 precompiles (CVE-2025-30147)
attack_steps: Forge an EC point P for the ALTBN128_ADD/MUL/PAIRING precompiles that sits in the right subgroup but not on the curve.
Craft a transaction with the forged point and include it in a block when a Besu node is the proposer.
Besu 24.7.1–25.2.2 accepts the block because it lacks a curve check, while other clients reject it as invalid.
The network can split between Besu nodes and other implementations, leaving Besu-only deployments on an invalid chain.
checkpoint: Ensure `point.IsOnCurve()` is invoked in the besu-native bn254 (`libbn254_*`) code instead of relying solely on subgroup checks, and add CL/EL integration tests that feed off-curve-but-in-subgroup points to verify hash agreement.

attack_title: Gas allocation error in CALL / DELEGATECALL (CVE-2022-36025)
attack_steps: Deploy a contract whose CALL/DELEGATECALL operations consume gas near the 2^31 threshold.
Trigger an internal CALL with a crafted transaction so that a 32-bit to 64-bit conversion bug treats a negative gas value as positive.
Besu continues execution with the inflated gas and updates the state root, while other clients revert or abort with out-of-gas.
Blocks proposed by Besu are rejected elsewhere, creating a consensus divergence.
checkpoint: Review `EVM#calculateAvailableGas()` and CALL opcode handlers to ensure 64-bit safe math is applied, unit-test the `remainingGas < 0` guard, and confirm exceptions are raised on negative or overflowed gas.

attack_title: Slow transaction verification DoS (EL-2022-11)
attack_steps: Generate transactions full of high-cost opcodes such as EXP or large SHA3 and send them with very high gas prices.
After signature and intrinsic gas checks, Besu performs quadratic-time EVM prechecks that spike CPU usage.
The transaction pool saturates and block verification stalls, prompting peers to disconnect the victim node.
The wider network advances, but the targeted Besu node desynchronises and is effectively DoSed.
checkpoint: Audit loops in `TransactionValidator` and `TransactionPool` to keep them linear in input size, and fuzz-test guards like `maxInitCodeSize` to ensure size limits stay effective.

attack_title: Exception during block import leads to chain split (EL-2022-14)
attack_steps: Prepare a block that contains abnormally encoded RLP transactions and wait until a Besu node proposes it.
Besu throws an uncaught `IllegalArgumentException` inside `BlockImporter`, drops the block, and the proposer keeps rebroadcasting it.
Other implementations accept the block, so only Besu nodes stall at the tip and the chain bifurcates.
Besu continues to prefer its local fork, destabilising the network over time.
checkpoint: Ensure `BlockImporter.processBlock()` and `AbstractBlockProcessor` catch unchecked exceptions and translate them into error codes, and add unit tests so RLP helpers (e.g. `BytesValues.toShort()`) enforce bounds.

attack_title: Slow input (16 s) DoS via crafted calldata (EL-2022-15)
attack_steps: Submit compressed, obfuscated calldata larger than 32 KB that expands into expensive loops inside the EVM.
Although gas accounting remains correct, missing JVM JIT optimisations stretch execution beyond 16 seconds.
Filling a 30M gas block with these transactions prevents validation from finishing within a 12-second slot, so proposers miss attestations.
Sustained spam causes peers to mark the node as slow and disconnect, achieving a DoS.
checkpoint: Profile `EVM#execute()` to keep memory copy/expansion close to O(n), and micro-benchmark multi-threaded `--engine-threads` to ensure worker starvation does not occur.

attack_title: Very slow block execution (EL-2022-17)
attack_steps: Deploy a 200 KB contract combining SELFDESTRUCT loops with huge memory allocations.
Pack many CALLs into a single block to consume the full 30M gas limit.
Besu's Bonsai trie updates create heavy GC pressure and massive write sets, pushing execution past 30 seconds and missing the slot deadline.
The consensus layer receives `ENGINE_UNAVAILABLE`, so Besu proposes empty blocks and loses MEV opportunities.
checkpoint: Review `WorldStateUpdater.commit()` so Bonsai diff flushes scale with the number of updates, and add CI benchmarks to guarantee large write sets finish within four seconds.

attack_title: Consensus flaw during block processing (EL-2022-09)
attack_steps: Wait until the attacker controls block proposal or operate on a private chain.
Insert two crafted transactions (SELF-DESTRUCT followed by CREATE and balance manipulation) into the block.
Erigon up to v2.37.0 copies the stale balance for a deleted account, producing a divergent state root.
Other clients compute the correct state root and reject the block, while Erigon continues on a forked chain.
checkpoint: Verify that `CreateAccount()` in core/state/statedb.go checks `prev.deleted` before copying balances, and add the Hive EVM test `SELFDESTRUCT_Create_ThenCall` to CI to detect state-root drift.

attack_title: Node stall from endless bad_block_hash retries (Gnosischain incident, 2023-02-28)
attack_steps: Produce an invalid block (for example with a malformed transaction) and deliver it to Erigon nodes, as happened on Gnosis Chain.
Erigon 2.39.x detects `bad_block_hash=0x00…00` during staged sync but keeps retrying the same stage instead of unwinding.
Header synchronisation halts and Execution↔Consensus pings fail, causing the beacon node to disconnect the client.
Restarting replays the loop, so the DoS persists until the bad block is cleared.
checkpoint: Ensure staged sync returns `StageResult{Unwind: true}` once a bad block is found (cf. PR #7025) and cover it with `integration/sync_bad_block.yaml`.

attack_title: DoS via malicious p2p message (invalid EC key)
attack_steps: Connect to a victim Geth node and initiate the ETH p2p handshake.
Send an Auth Hello packet whose EC public key is the all-zero point.
Geth attempts to derive a shared secret without validating that the key lies on secp256k1, triggering a panic.
The node crashes and disconnects from the network.
checkpoint: Confirm `elliptic.IsOnCurve(pub.X, pub.Y)` (or equivalent) is executed during the p2p handshake so zero or off-curve points are rejected.

attack_title: DoS via malicious GetBlockHeaders(count=0) integer underflow
attack_steps: Establish an ETH protocol peer connection and send `GetBlockHeaders` with `count` set to 0.
The calculation `count-1` underflows to UINT64_MAX and is passed to `GetHeadersFrom()`.
Geth attempts to stream essentially all headers despite `maxHeadersServe`, exhausting memory.
The victim node hits OOM and becomes unresponsive or crashes.
checkpoint: Reject `count==0` in `eth/protocol/handler.go` and guard the `count-1` arithmetic against underflow before calling header retrieval routines.

attack_title: Ping flood goroutine exhaustion DoS
attack_steps: Flood a victim with high-rate `Ping` messages over the p2p protocol.
Legacy Geth spawned a new goroutine for each ping.
Tens of thousands of goroutines accumulated, exhausting heap and scheduler resources.
The node eventually OOMs or stalls, realising a DoS.
checkpoint: Ensure the Ping/Pong handler reuses workers or rate-limits requests so that receiving pings no longer spawns unbounded goroutines.

attack_title: GraphQL endpoint massive query memory exhaustion DoS (CVE-2023-42319)
attack_steps: Target a node with `--http --graphql` enabled using HTTP.
Send a single query containing hundreds of heavy sub-queries combined with aliases.
Parsing and executing the query consumes gigabytes of heap until GC fails to keep up.
The node swaps or OOMs, leading to a crash and service interruption.
checkpoint: Add limits on per-request query size, AST nodes, and response payload in `graphql/http_handler.go`, and enable query cost rules such as `DepthLimit`.

attack_title: Gethlighting — partitioning attack via partial eclipse
attack_steps: Stand up dozens of VPS nodes and occupy roughly half of the victim's peer slots.
Delay block propagation by the malicious peers by 2–3 seconds (a low-rate DoS).
Honest blocks arrive too late via the remaining peers, so the victim's local chain stops advancing.
The victim remains stuck on an outdated tip for hours, effectively partitioned from the network.
checkpoint: Enforce /16 IP or AS-level peer limits in `p2p/peer.go`, randomise outbound peers, and drop connections whose propagation delay exceeds the configured threshold.

attack_title: DETER-X — future transaction abuse to exhaust the txpool
attack_steps: Create fresh accounts with zero ETH balance.
Send large numbers of high-gas-price transactions starting at nonce 1000 or higher (future nonces).
The txpool prioritises high gas price entries and evicts normal transactions, filling its capacity with unusable future txs.
Legitimate users cannot insert transactions and miners may mine empty blocks, cutting throughput.
checkpoint: Ensure `MaxFutureNonceGap` and per-sender limits (`txsPerSender`) are enforced in `txpool/tx_pool.go`, and add eviction logic so stale future transactions are purged after several blocks.

attack_title: BlocksByRange "count" overflow DoS (CL-2023-01)
attack_steps: Connect over the beacon p2p network to the victim.
Send a BlocksByRange request with an arbitrary start slot and an enormous count such as 4,294,967,295.
Lighthouse v4.4.0 and earlier cast count to `usize`, attempt to allocate a massive vector, and panic.
Simultaneously targeting many nodes halts network sync.
checkpoint: Ensure `handle_blocks_by_range()` in network/rpc/requests.rs enforces `count <= MAX_BLOCKS_BY_RANGE` and add fuzz tests covering values above 2^32.

attack_title: Electra Max-EB double application skews total_active_balance (#7209)
attack_steps: Prepare validator A with 2048 ETH eligible for consolidation and submit two stake transfer transactions across consecutive epochs.
Prior to v7.0.0-beta.4, Lighthouse called `update_effective_balance()` twice per consolidation because of a logic bug.
Validator A's effective balance doubles, inflating fork-choice total_active_balance.
Reward accounting diverges from other clients, risking a consensus split.
checkpoint: Add a unit test ensuring `update_effective_balance()` only runs once per validator per epoch and cover the consolidation flow with regression tests.

attack_title: Accepting stale fork attestations skews fork choice (#7084)
attack_steps: Broadcast a flood of attestations targeting blocks on an old fork after a chain split.
Lighthouse v7.0.0-beta.3 lacked the split_slot check and accepted the attestations.
Fork choice favours the stale branch, delaying new block proposals and halting finality.
Sustained abuse can trap Lighthouse nodes on the wrong fork.
checkpoint: Update `check_target_fork()` in attestation_verification.rs to mark `target.slot < split_slot` as invalid and cover it with tests.

attack_title: Holesky accepts an invalid justified block (#7041)
attack_steps: Submit a block on Holesky carrying an invalid execution payload and tampered justified checkpoint.
Lighthouse v7.0.0-beta.0 through beta.2 failed to blacklist the invalid block root and treated it as justified.
Lighthouse validators stop finalising, while other clients ignore the block.
Attackers can weaponise the stall for MEV capture or censorship.
checkpoint: Ensure `fork_choice::on_block()` rejects known-invalid block roots and immediately bans the peer broadcasting them.

attack_title: Light-client Merkle proof misgeneration stalls sync (#7007)
attack_steps: Issue high-frequency `/light_client/updates` requests from many light clients.
Versions v6.1.x to v7.0.0-beta.0 returned inconsistent proofs due to a hot-state cache mismatch.
Light clients fail verification and halt synchronisation, while server CPU usage climbs.
Large request volumes can also tax the beacon node.
checkpoint: Add SSZ-fuzz coverage to `light_client::builder::build_merkle_proof()` ensuring state variants align with proof paths, including EIP-4788 block-root fixtures.

attack_title: Attestation subscription regression raises repeated InsufficientPeers (#6869)
attack_steps: Rapidly toggle gossipsub subnet subscriptions in the network.
In v7.0.0, a bug miscounted attestation subscriptions and validators logged `InsufficientPeers` repeatedly.
Block proposal preparation is skipped, causing missed proposals and attestations.
Log spam and CPU churn produce a mild DoS.
checkpoint: Ensure `subscribe_all_subnets()` in network::attestation_service clamps peer counts correctly and add regression tests for the subscription accounting.

attack_title: Unfinalized BlocksByRange root recalculation DoS (#7098)
attack_steps: Send repeated BlocksByRange requests covering large spans of unfinalized slots.
Before v7.0.0-beta.5, Lighthouse reran fork choice for each request to compute roots, saturating CPU.
Response latency spikes and sync delays build up.
Parallel requests can exhaust resources and crash the node.
checkpoint: Cache fork-choice results or reject ranges outside finalised scope in `rpc::block_by_range()`, and cover the behaviour with regression tests.

attack_title: SMOD opcode signed remainder edge-case bug (EL-2022-10)
attack_steps: Deploy a contract that executes `SMOD` with `int256.min` (−2^255) as the operand.
Nethermind 1.17.x–1.18.x double-negates the value, producing a result with the wrong sign compared to other clients.
Including that transaction in a block yields a different state root on Nethermind, creating consensus divergence.
Syncing nodes extend a net-invalid chain, risking a full chain split.
checkpoint: Add unit tests around `int256.min` in the EVM implementation (`Smod.cs`), and use static analysis to confirm `BigInteger.Abs()` is not followed by an extra sign flip.

attack_title: ModExp precompile out-of-memory consensus failure (EL-2022-13)
attack_steps: Generate a payload with `expLength = 0xffffffff` while keeping `baseLength = modulusLength = 0`.
Other clients return success early when modulus == 0, but Nethermind allocates memory first, hits `OutOfMemory`, and returns `success=false`.
The block results differ only on Nethermind, so its block hash diverges.
If a Nethermind validator proposes the block, the network forks; otherwise Nethermind nodes follow a minority chain and incur penalties.
checkpoint: Ensure `ModExpPrecompile.Run()` evaluates early-return conditions before allocating memory, and add tests verifying allocations beyond `Array.MaxLength` are caught.

attack_title: Slow-input (6 s) DoS via massive calldata expansion (EL-2022-16)
attack_steps: Deploy a contract whose obfuscated calldata of just over 32 KB expands linearly in an EVM loop.
Pack several such transactions into a 30M gas block so `InitCodeParser` spends more than six seconds, eating half the 12 s slot.
Execution→Consensus responses time out, so the beacon node rejects the proposals or produces empty blocks.
Sustained spam gets the victim disconnected and forces resynchronisation.
checkpoint: Profile `TransactionValidator.Validate()` and `Evm.BytecodeAnalyzer` to ensure loops stay O(n), and verify calldata length and iteration caps are enforced (`EvmLimits`).

attack_title: Revert message length overflow yields invalid blocks (Jan 2024 hotfix)
attack_steps: Send a transaction whose `revert()` returns a very large data payload.
Nethermind 1.23–1.25 throws `OverflowException` in the revert decoder and marks the transaction as failed while other clients succeed.
Gas accounting diverges within the proposed block, so Nethermind's block hash differs and it drops off the chain.
Validators lose attestations and accumulate penalties while the attacker isolates Nethermind nodes without affecting the rest of the network.
checkpoint: Upgrade `RevertDecoder.Decode()` to use `uint64` lengths with bounds checks, and fuzz the decoder to prove overflow paths are eliminated without relying on `try/catch`.

attack_title: BLS precompile optimisation bug triggered Devnet 5 fork (2025-01-30)
attack_steps: Pectra Devnet 5 enabled a performance patch for the BLS12-381 VERIFY precompile.
Submitting an invalid signature edge case leads Nethermind to treat the verification as successful.
Other clients reject the block, so only Nethermind nodes follow the faulty chain and finality stalls.
The issue was rolled back within hours on the devnet but would cause a mainnet split.
checkpoint: Ensure `BlsPrecompile.Verify()` fast paths run the same pairing checks as the fallback, and add Hive/EOF tests so forged signatures never pass.

attack_title: Electra epoch processing updates effective balance incorrectly (v6.0.4)
attack_steps: On a post-Electra chain, an attacker operates several large validators (>32 ETH) under normal conditions.
They issue heavy deposits and withdrawals around epoch boundaries to swing the actual balance sharply.
Prysm ≤6.0.3 copied validator structs with unsafe pointer semantics, so `effective_balance` kept the stale value.
Total active balance diverges from other clients, rewards and fork choice misalign, finality stalls, and Prysm nodes leak for extended periods.
checkpoint: Ensure `updateEffectiveBalance()` in core/state/epoch_processing.go copies structs by value and applies the Electra hysteresis correctly for balances above 32 ETH; cover the path with targeted unit tests.

attack_title: Nested slashing DB path breaks history export (v6.0.4)
attack_steps: Convince the operator to place `validator.db` inside a nested directory (e.g., `data/direct/`).
When the operator runs `prysmctl slashing-protection-history export`, a path resolution bug emits an empty file.
Importing the empty DB on another node removes protection, making double proposals or attestations likely and leading to slashing.
The attacker profits from penalties incurred by the victim validators.
checkpoint: Normalise export paths to absolute locations with `filepath.Abs()`/`os.MkdirAll()` in validator/db/export.go and add tests covering nested directory layouts to ensure non-empty exports.

attack_title: Beacon API committees endpoint mishandles invalid slots (v6.0.4)
attack_steps: Send waves of malformed slot values to `/eth/v1/beacon/states/{state_id}/committees` via UI or bots.
Prysm ≤6.0.3 logged a panic and produced HTTP 500 responses instead of 400 errors.
Sustained traffic forces the Beacon API into restart loops, achieving a DoS.
checkpoint: Have the committees handler in beacon/api/server.go validate `slot / spec.SLOTS_PER_EPOCH` and return HTTP 400 immediately; fuzz boundary values to verify graceful handling.

attack_title: Pending consolidations endpoint handler panic (v6.0.3)
attack_steps: Continuously poll `/eth/v1/beacon/pool/pending_consolidations`.
Prysm ≤6.0.2 returned `nil`, causing JSON marshaling to panic and resetting HTTP connections.
Many concurrent connections push the container into a crash loop and blind monitoring.
checkpoint: Ensure beacon/api/consolidations.go returns an empty array when the slice is empty and add unit tests covering the nil case.

attack_title: Attester slashing pool API returns nil (v6.0.3)
attack_steps: Send repeated REST queries from monitoring tools that expect array responses.
Prysm ≤6.0.2 emitted `nil`, causing JSON deserialisers to throw errors and crash watchdogs.
With monitoring offline, an attacker can submit malicious attestations without timely detection.
checkpoint: Have beacon/api/slashings.go always return an array type, update the OpenAPI schema to disallow `null`, and add regression tests for empty responses.

attack_title: Validator participation API ignores state_id (v6.0.3)
attack_steps: Query `state_id` using historical block roots to feed misleading statistics to dashboards.
Prysm ≤6.0.2 ignored the parameter and always returned HEAD data, masking forks.
Attackers can lure validators into forked chains by presenting healthy metrics.
checkpoint: Verify router/validators.go validates `state_id` inputs and that `ResolveStateID()` returns 404 when the identifier is unknown; add regression tests.

attack_title: Pending partial withdrawals JSON tag typo (v6.0.2)
attack_steps: Rely on explorer dashboards that parse pending withdrawal fields.
Name mismatches lead to incorrect balances being displayed, prompting users to miscalculate fees or payouts.
While the chain data stays correct, UX degradation can cause financial mistakes.
checkpoint: Align JSON tags in beacon/api/types/withdrawals.go with the spec (`pending_partial_withdrawals`) and cover schema validation in tests.

attack_title: gocognit propose-block REST path misrouting (v6.0.2)
attack_steps: Send REST requests to an incorrect builder path such as `/eth/v2/beacon/blocks/proper/`.
Prysm ≤6.0.1 returned HTTP 500 instead of 404 and dropped Keep-Alive connections, delaying block proposals.
Targeted spam near slot deadlines causes proposal misses.
checkpoint: Normalise the `/eth/v2/beacon/blocks/{block_id}` routes in the mux configuration and add tests to ensure invalid paths return `http.StatusNotFound`.

attack_title: `payload_attributes` SSE stream underflow (v6.0.1)
attack_steps: Have an MEV-Boost relay flood the `payload_attributes` SSE stream with events.
Prysm ≤6.0.0 hit a ring-buffer underflow and silently dropped subsequent events, ignoring builder bids.
Victim validators lose MEV revenue while attackers prioritise their own blocks.
checkpoint: Add back-pressure in eventstream/payload_attributes.go so publishing beyond the channel capacity is detected, and fuzz with >1k events/s to ensure no panic occurs.

attack_title: Leaking edge case causes balance underflow and negative rewards (v6.0.1)
attack_steps: Maintain a prolonged non-finalising (leak) condition to drive some validators' balances down.
Prysm ≤6.0.0 experienced `uint64` underflow, adding 2^64−X during reward processing before normalisation.
Prysm nodes compute divergent block hashes and form an isolated chain, incurring penalties.
checkpoint: Use `safeMath.SubClamp()` (or equivalent) in processRewards.go, and add CI tests covering leak scenarios to prevent underflow.

attack_title: Critical chain-reorg logic bug (v1.4.8)
attack_steps: Identify validators or RPC nodes running Reth ≤1.4.7.
Create competing short reorgs near the tip, staying within the persistence threshold.
A fork-choice bug fails to recompute trie updates correctly, canonising the wrong state root.
Affected nodes diverge from the network and incur penalties or downtime.
checkpoint: Test `engine::reorg::recompute_trie_updates()` to ensure every forked block recalculates trie paths, and cover the `ParallelSparseTrie` prefix updates fixed in #16568.

attack_title: OP Mainnet gas refund mismatch causes chain split (v1.4.7)
attack_steps: Propose blocks on Optimism Mainnet packed with maximum `SSTORE` gas refunds.
Reth ≤1.4.6 underestimates refunded gas and miscomputes `gasUsed`.
Reth nodes mark the block invalid and fork away from op-geth implementations.
Sequencers or RPC nodes lose sync and halt service.
checkpoint: Unit-test `optimism::gas_refund::apply_refund()` across EIP-3529/EIP-3855 cases and add high-refund transactions to ensure `gasUsed` matches other clients.

attack_title: Stack overflow DoS via malformed block (#6452)
attack_steps: As a validator, craft a block containing invalid transactions and broadcast it.
Reth ≤0.1.0-alpha.20 recurses in `BlockchainTree::insert_block()` until the stack overflows.
The node panics and crashes; upon restart it reprocesses the same block and loops.
checkpoint: Ensure bad-block handling breaks recursion and returns errors early, and fuzz block validation to prove infinite recursion cannot occur.

attack_title: Invalid state-root retry loop crashes node (#16113)
attack_steps: Craft a block with tampered receipts so the state root differs from the actual value.
Reth ~1.3.12 re-attempted the same block without unwinding after `ExecutionStage::apply_block()` failed.
The sync pipeline loops until a fatal mismatched state-root error stops the process.
Lengthy resync is required, taking validators or RPC services offline.
checkpoint: Make sure `apply_block()` propagates `StageError::BadBlock` upward so the unwind stage always runs; cover the logic in unit tests.

attack_title: Massive RPC reads sever beacon connection DoS (#17323)
attack_steps: Hammer an archive Reth 1.5.x node with highly parallel `eth_call` requests over IPC/HTTP.
`Database::reader()` holds locks for minutes and throws `Long-lived read transaction timed out`.
Engine API responses to the beacon client time out, severing the EL↔CL link.
The node misses proposals, loses rewards, and becomes unavailable.
checkpoint: Tighten `db::TX_READ_TIMEOUT` or add cooperative cancellation for reader transactions, and load-test (e.g., tokio + alloy) at 10k rps to ensure beacon pings stay healthy.

attack_title: KZG library accepts invalid elliptic-curve points
affected_client: Fusaka Devnet: clients using go-kzg-4844 (e.g., Geth, Nethermind)
attack_steps: A malicious proposer crafts a block containing blob commitments with invalid G1/G2 points.
Implementations missing strict decoding accept the block while others reject it, potentially splitting the chain.
technical_details: KZG commitment verification failed to reject malformed BLS12-381 points, opening the door to consensus splits if clients disagreed on commitment validity.
cve_or_bug_report: Sigma Prime audit finding; no CVE assigned
fix: Point deserialisation checks were hardened in gnark-crypto v0.11.2 and downstream clients updated to the patched library.
checkpoint: Add Sigma Prime's reproduction vectors to CI and ensure gnark-crypto v0.11.2+ errors on invalid G1/G2 points.

attack_title: ForkID mismatch rejects peers
attack_steps: No attacker required: Besu computed an incorrect ForkID and voluntarily disconnected healthy peers.
An attacker could exploit the bug to isolate Besu nodes.
technical_details: During Fusaka Devnet 2, Besu derived ForkIDs that deviated from the spec, marking valid peers as invalid and lowering finality participation.
fix: ForkID computation was realigned with EIP-2124 and rolled out before Devnet-3 alongside a shared `eth_config` to prevent regressions.
checkpoint: Load the Fusaka `eth_config` and verify ForkHash/ForkNext alignment with EIP-2124 through devp2p handshake and multi-client interoperability tests.

attack_title: Four-byte chain ID parsing disconnects peers
attack_steps: Guide Nethermind onto a network whose chain ID length differs from expectations (e.g., 4-byte IDs).
The node immediately drops peers, degrading synchronisation; attackers could weaponise misconfigured IDs to cause desync.
technical_details: Nethermind misparsed 4-byte chain IDs during the devp2p handshake, causing repeated disconnects right after connection establishment.
fix: Chain ID handling now supports IDs of four bytes or longer, shipped ahead of Devnet-3 in a Nethermind update.
checkpoint: Handshake with a chain ID such as `0x0000fusa` and ensure Nethermind maintains the connection and syncs in integration tests.

attack_title: Beacon root validation failure blocks sync
attack_steps: Although no concrete exploit is known, a hostile peer could feed malformed roots to keep Nimbus dropping connections.
technical_details: Nimbus EL/CL combined clients dropped peers due to beacon-root validation errors, leaving Devnet-2 participants effectively offline.
fix: Nimbus revised the validation path so subsequent devnets synchronised normally after applying the update.
checkpoint: Extend beacon block import tests with Fusaka fixtures to assert invalid roots are rejected while valid ones keep syncing.

attack_title: Checkpoint sync DB inconsistency
attack_steps: After prolonged non-finality, repeatedly restart Prysm with an existing database to trigger checkpoint sync failure.
technical_details: Restarting in a non-finalised state with checkpoint sync enabled produced database mismatches when the justified and finalised checkpoints were far apart.
fix: Prysm adjusted database initialisation and checkpoint application logic so restart-from-checkpoint succeeds with existing data.
checkpoint: Add an end-to-end test that restarts Prysm with a populated DB after non-finality and confirms checkpoint sync completes.

attack_title: Large data requests cause Teku OOM
attack_steps: Spam BlobSidecars-by-root requests (e.g., batches of 512) over gossip to exhaust Teku's memory.
technical_details: Teku lacked rate controls for excessive PeerDAS column requests; malicious peers drove memory usage high enough to crash nodes during Devnet-3.
fix: Teku introduced rate limits that ignore or deprioritise abusive requests, tracked under Teku issue #9824 and shipped in the latest release.
checkpoint: Run PeerDAS tests that issue 512 BlobSidecars requests and confirm rate limiting stabilises memory usage.

attack_title: Data-availability subnet loses peers
attack_steps: Overload Lighthouse with custody subnet management so it falls behind on required data pieces, risking delayed chain following.
technical_details: Lighthouse struggled to maintain peers across custody subnets and stalled when encountering forks, cutting participation to ~50% during Devnet-3.
fix: Peer scoring and connection maintenance improvements are in progress; patches are planned for the next release cycle to stabilise custody management.
checkpoint: Add integration tests that monitor custody subnet peer counts and assert they remain above the target threshold over time.

attack_title: Data column index bug returns wrong blobs
attack_steps: Coerce Lodestar into requesting specific columns so it serves incorrect data, provoking sync delays or validation errors.
technical_details: Lodestar's data-availability column index was mis-implemented, occasionally returning the wrong column and lacking support for finalised-epoch lookups by root.
fix: PeerDAS improvements—larger peer counts, faster getBlobsV2 deserialisation, and a pending fix for the column index—are rolling out in upcoming releases.
checkpoint: Exercise the PeerDAS API with known column requests and verify the returned data roots match expected hashes in unit and scenario tests.

attack_title: Blob fee comparison bug
attack_steps: Feed Reth crafted blob transactions so only Reth fails to build blocks while other clients succeed.
technical_details: Reth's EIP-4844 blob gas fee comparison logic was incorrect, causing discrepancies with other clients and failed block construction during testing.
fix: PR #18216 corrected the conditionals, landing in the latest Reth release and aligning blob fee calculations.
checkpoint: Add engine tests that compare blob fee results across clients and assert zero difference in max blob fee.

attack_title: Trie root mismatch during deep reorg
attack_steps: Trigger repeated deep reorgs so Erigon rejects new payloads with trie root mismatches, halting block processing.
technical_details: Erigon reported `invalid block…wrong trie root` during deep reorgs; payloads fetched via the engine API were rejected until the database was wiped, indicating inconsistent internal state.
fix: Erigon is refining reorg handling; fixes for known cases are implemented and undergoing internal testing before release.
checkpoint: Simulate reorgs deeper than five blocks in Hive tests to confirm Erigon accepts payloads with the correct trie root.

attack_title: History truncation corrupts database
attack_steps: Encourage frequent restarts of history-expiry-enabled nodes to repeatedly trigger the failure and crash them.
technical_details: When trimming historical state during restart, Geth reported `history head truncation out of range…` and halted block processing, especially on nodes synced from genesis.
fix: Boundary checks in the history trimming logic were corrected in Geth v1.13.x, resolving the inconsistency.
checkpoint: Add a regression test that restarts a history-expiry-enabled node and asserts no `history head truncation` errors appear in the logs.

attack_title: Snap sync stalls
attack_steps: Although no deliberate exploit was documented, a malicious engine could return empty payload lists to hang snap-synchronising nodes.
technical_details: Devnet testing showed snap sync halting at specific heights when payloads were interpreted as empty, while full sync continued normally.
fix: Geth adjusted snap sync logic to match full-sync reliability; current development builds no longer stall.
checkpoint: Mock empty payload responses at height N in snap-sync E2E tests and verify sync continues after the fix.

attack_title: Deep reorg fork-choice check missing
attack_steps: Force extreme consecutive reorgs so Nethermind selects the wrong parent, halting chain updates.
technical_details: Nethermind selected incorrect parents during deep reorgs, failed internal validation, and paused chain progression; the stack was reproducible in internal tests.
fix: Parent selection logic is being reworked; patches are under test and will ship once stabilised.
checkpoint: Add deep reorg scenarios to fork-choice tests and confirm Nethermind rolls back to the correct parent with diagnostic logs.
